title: FCW Persistent-State Schema (fcw-persistent-state-v1.0)
description: Schema for the FIMI/Cognitive-Warfare monitor's `persistent-state.json` — the canonical knowledge-base file the Applier writes to. The MVP shape is intentionally permissive on per-module structure (`additionalProperties: true` everywhere outside `_meta`); per-module strictness is the responsibility of MBC sweeps. The strict layer is the `_meta` block — `schema_version`, `last_updated`, `last_issue`, `monitor_slug`. Sprint-5 Phase 3 (BRIEF cf14eabd, E10 cure) adds a top-level `arrays` block declaring, for every persistent-state array FCW cares about, the merge `key_field` and a `key_constraint` (regex pattern OR string enum). The engine (`pipeline.engine.persistent_state.apply_patches_to_persistent_state`, amended in Phase 1) reads these declarations to enforce additive merge semantics and raise `DisjointKeySchemeError` on key-scheme rotation. Authority: BRIEF-SPRINT-5-PERSISTENT-STATE-KEY-LOCK §Phase 3; AGM precedent PR #338 (cb6fd959).

schema_version.description: Schema version of this file. Canonical value: `fcw-persistent-state-v1.0`.

last_updated.description: Most recent material state change (ISO date YYYY-MM-DD or full ISO-8601 timestamp). Bumped by the Applier on cycles that land at least one patch; NOT bumped on null cycles.

last_issue.description: Issue counter. The Applier increments this by 1 on cycles that land at least one patch when the current value is an integer.

module_2.campaigns.describe: Active FIMI campaign entries in the interpreter output (module_2). `id` is the canonical campaign identifier following the actor-prefix scheme (e.g. `RU-001`, `CN-002`, `GULF-UAE-001`, `AI-NET-001`). Pattern-locked against the live scheme; enum lock is impractical given open-ended actor/numeric combinations.

module_6.items.describe: Strategic intelligence items (module_6 — doctrine shifts, capability deployments, strategic patterns, narrative operations, epistemic attacks). `id` is the canonical item identifier. Free-form pattern — item ids are cycle-scoped and do not follow a fixed scheme in the interpreter-schema.

key_judgments.describe: FCW key-judgments array declared in the interpreter-schema. Pattern-locked against the FCW-KJ-NNN scheme to prevent the Interpreter from rotating to free-form judgment ids mid-cycle (E10 pathology).

infrastructure_registry.entries.describe: Named FIMI infrastructure entity registry entries (state contractors, hosting providers, front organisations). `id` follows the INFRA-NNN scheme as established in live persistent-state (INFRA-001, INFRA-002...). Pattern-lock prevents identifier rotation at merge time.

platform_enforcement_tracker.describe: Platform enforcement action tracker — each entry records one CIB/takedown/DSA enforcement action. `id` follows the PET-NNN scheme as established in live persistent-state. Forward-declared here so engine merge semantics apply automatically once the Applier targets this key.

calibration_log.describe: Append-only calibration log (one entry per material methodology change). `date` is ISO date or full ISO-8601 timestamp. The engine treats this array as additive-only by construction. BRIEF §3d required route.

gaps_register.describe: Open evidence-gap register (FCW). `gap_id` follows the GAP-NNN scheme as established in live persistent-state. Pattern-lock prevents the Interpreter from rotating to free-form gap ids (e.g. FCW-GAP-001, gap-001).
